name: Kahlua White Russian Cake
recipeIngredient: 3 ounces White Baking Bar with Cocoa, shaved into curls (use a potato peeler)
recipeInstructions:
-------------------------KAHLUA WHITE RUSSIAN CREME-------------------------
1. In a medium heavy saucepan, combine Kahlua, vodka, and chopped White Baking Bar with Cocoa. Cook and stir over low heat until the baking bar is melted, then cool slightly.
2. Grease and lightly flour three 8x1 1/2 round baking pans (I use my 9-inch pans).
3. In a small bowl, stir together flour, baking soda, and baking powder. Set aside.
4. In a large bowl, combine sugar, butter, and shortening. Beat with an electric mixer on medium speed until light and fluffy.
5. Add eggs one at a time, beating until combined. Beat in the cooled liqueur mixture.
6. Alternately add the flour mixture and buttermilk to the egg mixture, beating on low to medium speed after each addition until combined.
7. Spread the batter evenly in pans.
8. Bake at 350°F for 20-25 minutes or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean.
9. Cool the cake layers in the pans on wire racks for 10 minutes. Remove from pans and cool completely on wire racks.
10. To assemble, place one layer, bottom side up, on a serving plate. Spread the layer with about 3 tablespoons of the jam and 1/4 cup of the Kahlua White Russian Cream. Place a second layer on top of the first, bottom side up, and spread with the remaining jam and cream. Top with the remaining cake layer, bottom side up.
11. Swirl the remaining cream onto the top and sides of the cake. Decorate the top (I also decorate the sides, which looks so pretty!) with baking bar curls.
12. Cover and chill for up to 24 hours before serving.
Serving:
Cut into wedges using a dipping knife dipped in water between cuts. Serves 12-16.
